explanationHtml:
Step 1: Simplify the given equation.
$30x=1{,}800y-3{,}600$ → divide by 30 → $x=60y-120$.
Rearranged: $x-60y=-120$. The slope of $y$ as a function of $x$ is $ rac{1}{60}$.

Step 2: No-solution condition.
For no solution, the second equation must be parallel: same slope ($ rac{1}{60}$) but different constant.

Step 3: Test all four choices.

  • Choice A — ‘$ rac{1}{30}x=2y$’: Multiply both sides by 30: $x=60y$, i.e. $x-60y=0$. Same slope $ rac{1}{60}$, different constant ($0 eq -120$). Parallel → no solution ✓
  • Choice B — ‘$ rac{1}{30}x=60y-120$’: Multiply by 30: $x=1{,}800y-3{,}600$. Slope $ rac{1}{1800} eq rac{1}{60}$ → lines intersect → one solution ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$x=2y$’: Slope of $y$ w.r.t. $x$ is $ rac{1}{2} eq rac{1}{60}$ → lines intersect → one solution ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$x=60y-120$’: Identical to the given equation → infinitely many solutions ✗

That matches choice A — A is correct.
